title: Disney Villainous
aliases: Disney Villainous: The Worst Takes It All

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player Boards & Movers
- **6 Villain Movers** — One mover for each villain.
- **6 Boards** — One realm board per villain.
- **3 Lock Tokens** — Used by villains whose right-most location starts locked.

#### Villain & Fate Cards
- **6 Villain Decks** — 30 cards in each deck.
- **6 Fate Decks** — 15 cards in each deck.
- **6 Villain Guides** — Character-specific rules and strategy notes.
- **6 Reference Cards** — Action-symbol reminder cards.

#### Shared Resources
- **80 Power Tokens** — Shared currency in the cauldron.
- **1 Cauldron** — Holds shared Power supply.
- **1 Fate Token** — Included component from the base set.

#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0)
- **Supported counts found** — 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6 players.
- **Count-specific setup found** — Starting Power by seat position (1st through 6th).
- **No solo mode found** — Provided excerpt contains no solo/solitaire rules.
- **No board-size scaling found** — No component removal table in provided excerpt.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Board placement** — Each player places their villain board in front of them.
2. **Mover start** — Place Villain Mover on the left-most location.
3. **Lock check** — If the right-most location shows a lock icon, place a Lock Token there.
4. **Villain deck lane** — Place Villain Deck face down to the left with room for discard.
5. **Fate deck lane** — Place Fate Deck face down to the right with room for Fate discard.
6. **Shared cauldron** — Put Cauldron with all 80 Power tokens where everyone can reach it.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Choose villain** — Take matching Board, Villain Mover, Villain Deck, Fate Deck, Villain Guide, and Reference Card.
- **Shuffle Villain Deck** — Keep deck face down in your play area.
- **Draw opening hand** — Draw 4 cards from your Villain Deck.
- **Hand privacy** — Keep your hand hidden from other players.
- **Shuffle Fate Deck** — Keep Fate Deck face down in your play area.
- **Objective reveal** — Read your board objective aloud so all players know your win condition.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **First player** — Choose a player to go first.

#### Starting Power by Seat Position
--- 2 Players ---
- **1st player** — Start with 0 Power.
- **2nd player** — Start with 1 Power.

--- 3 Players ---
- **1st player** — Start with 0 Power.
- **2nd player** — Start with 1 Power.
- **3rd player** — Start with 2 Power.

--- 4 Players ---
- **1st player** — Start with 0 Power.
- **2nd player** — Start with 1 Power.
- **3rd player** — Start with 2 Power.
- **4th player** — Start with 2 Power.

--- 5 Players ---
- **1st player** — Start with 0 Power.
- **2nd player** — Start with 1 Power.
- **3rd player** — Start with 2 Power.
- **4th player** — Start with 2 Power.
- **5th player** — Start with 3 Power.

--- 6 Players ---
- **1st player** — Start with 0 Power.
- **2nd player** — Start with 1 Power.
- **3rd player** — Start with 2 Power.
- **4th player** — Start with 2 Power.
- **5th player** — Start with 3 Power.
- **6th player** — Start with 3 Power.

- **Power source** — Take starting Power from the shared Cauldron.
- **First action** — First player begins by moving to a legal location and taking available actions.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Move Villain Mover** — Move to a different location in your realm.
2. **Use location actions** — Perform visible action symbols at that location in any order.
3. **Resolve card text** — Apply played/triggered effects from cards and abilities.
4. **Refill hand** — Draw up to 4 cards from your Villain Deck.
5. **Check objective timing** — Confirm whether your villain can win now or only at start of turn.
6. **Pass turn** — Play continues clockwise.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Core Action Symbols
- **Gain Power** — Take Power from the Cauldron equal to symbol value.
- **Play a Card** — Pay card cost and play it to a legal target/location.
- **Move an Item or Ally** — Relocate one eligible Item or Ally.
- **Move a Hero** — Move one Hero as allowed.
- **Activate** — Use an Activated Ability on a card in play, paying required costs.
- **Vanquish** — Defeat a Hero using legal ally-strength resolution.
- **Fate** — Target another player with their Fate deck effects.
- **Discard Cards** — Discard any number of cards from hand.

#### Action Access Rules
- **Location-gated actions** — You only use actions visible at your current location.
- **Covered symbols** — Heroes can cover symbols and block those actions.
- **Card exceptions** — If card text conflicts with baseline action flow, follow card text.
- **Extra symbols** — Some cards add action symbols at locations.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference List (Step 0)
- **2 players found** — Included in supported range and starting-power setup.
- **3 players found** — Included in supported range and starting-power setup.
- **4 players found** — Included in supported range and starting-power setup.
- **5 players found** — Included in supported range and starting-power setup.
- **6 players found** — Included in supported range and starting-power setup.
- **No solo text found** — No solo/solitaire rules in provided excerpt.
- **No count-based action changes found** — Action rules appear constant across 2-6 in provided excerpt.

#### Asymmetric Objectives (Per Villain)
- **Captain Hook** — Defeat Peter Pan at the Jolly Roger.
- **Jafar** — Start your turn with Magic Lamp at Sultan’s Palace and Genie under your control.
- **Maleficent** — Start your turn with a Curse at each location.
- **Prince John** — Start your turn with at least 20 Power.
- **Queen of Hearts** — Have a Wicket at each location and successfully take a shot.
- **Ursula** — Start your turn with the Trident and Crown at Ursula’s Lair.

#### Timing Notes
- **Asterisk timing** — Some villains can only win at the start of their turn.
- **Mid-turn caveat** — Meeting board state mid-turn may not win immediately if start-of-turn wording applies.

#### Interaction Systems
- **Fate pressure** — Players can slow leaders by adding Heroes/effects to opponent realms.
- **Lock pressure** — Some realms begin with right-most location locked.
- **Guide dependency** — Villain Guide text is part of proper teaching for that character.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
- **End trigger** — Game ends immediately when a player fulfills their objective with correct timing.

#### Win Conditions in This Base Set
- **Captain Hook win** — Complete Hook objective condition.
- **Jafar win** — Complete Jafar objective condition.
- **Maleficent win** — Complete Maleficent objective condition.
- **Prince John win** — Complete Prince John objective condition.
- **Queen of Hearts win** — Complete Queen of Hearts objective condition.
- **Ursula win** — Complete Ursula objective condition.

#### Scoring Model
- **No VP track** — Villainous does not use victory points to determine winner.
- **No final tally** — First legal objective completion wins instantly.

#### Tie Handling
- **Typical outcome** — Simultaneous wins are uncommon due to turn structure.
- **Resolution approach** — Use turn order and card timing hierarchy from rules if ambiguity appears.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Power first** — Prioritize early locations that produce stable Power income.
- **Objective mapping** — Identify which 2-3 cards or board states your villain needs to win.
- **Hand cycling** — Use discard actions early to find engine cards faster.
- **Tempo concealment** — Advance in ways that do not overexpose you to Fate focus too soon.
- **Timing discipline** — If your objective is start-of-turn, build a board state that survives one full table cycle.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Value over objective** — Playing efficient cards that do not advance your win condition slows your race.
- **Static movement** — Failing to plan next-turn location access creates dead turns.
- **Card hoarding** — Keeping weak hands without cycling delays critical combo pieces.
- **No Fate usage** — Ignoring leader disruption lets opponents win uncontested.
- **Timing misread** — Assuming start-of-turn objectives can score mid-turn causes preventable losses.
- **Over-clearing threats** — Spending too many actions fixing minor problems can stall your own objective clock.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Race vs disrupt** — Decide when to advance your board versus spending Fate to slow the leader.
- **Power now vs setup later** — Choose between immediate income actions and lower-income setup turns.
- **Hold vs pitch cards** — Keep situational combo cards or discard to improve draw velocity.
- **Visible threat vs hidden line** — Show fast progress and attract Fate, or build quieter lines before revealing finish.

metadata.notes: Primary source: Villainous text provided in prompt (component counts, setup sequence, action symbols, objective/timing statements).
